The iron never lies to you.
Two hundred pounds is always two hundred pounds.
The iron is the great reference point, the all-knowing perspective giver.
It wasn't until my late twenties that I learned that by working out I had given myself a great gift.
I used to fight the pain, but recently this became clear to me: pain is not my enemy; it is my call to greatness.
I have never met a truly strong person who didn't have self-respect.
Strength is kindness and sensitivity. Strength is understanding that your power is both physical and emotional.
Through the years, I have combined meditation, action, and the iron into a single strength.
The iron had taught me how to live.
